title: Contrastive Novelty Learning: Anticipating Outliers with Large Language Models

structured.summary: This paper proposes CoNAL, a novel framework leveraging large language models to generate semantically novel out-of-distribution (OOD) classes and corresponding examples, coupled with a contrastive confidence loss to train classifiers that abstain on OOD inputs. Experiments show improved open-set selective classification and OOD detection performance across multiple NLP datasets without degrading in-distribution accuracy.

strengths.point: Innovative use of LLMs for generating novel OOD labels and examples
strengths.evidence: CoNAL's Novelty Prompting stage uses GPT-3 and GPT-J to create semantically novel and plausible novel class labels and examples, enabling better OOD representation without requiring real OOD data.

strengths.point: Contrastive Confidence Loss effectively improves OOD abstention
strengths.evidence: CCL encourages the model to have relatively lower confidence on generated novel examples than in-distribution examples, outperforming prior methods like Outlier Exposure and ensembles while maintaining in-distribution accuracy.

strengths.point: Comprehensive evaluation across diverse datasets and settings
strengths.evidence: Experiments on four NLP datasets with multiple open-set splits demonstrate consistent gains in OOD detection (5.5% AUROC improvement) and OSSC accuracy (2.3% AUAC improvement), with robustness analyses on generation noise, LLM size, and generation budgets.

weaknesses.point: Lack of comparisons to recent NLP-specific OOD detection methods
weaknesses.evidence: The paper omits comparisons to energy-based, Mahalanobis distance, spectral normalization, and other contemporary NLP OOD detection approaches, weakening claims of state-of-the-art performance.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient ablation studies on key components and hyperparameters
weaknesses.evidence: No ablation or quantitative analysis is provided on the impact of synonym filtering during novel label generation, contrastive loss hyperparameters, or the size and quality of generated novel examples.

weaknesses.point: Dependence on GPT-3 API raises reproducibility and stability concerns
weaknesses.evidence: Reliance on GPT-3 for label generation involves opaque, evolving model behavior and training data, complicating exact replication and wider adoption.

weaknesses.point: Limited dataset diversity and artificial open-set splits constrain generalizability
weaknesses.evidence: Experiments use only four datasets with artificial splits that may not reflect real-world open-set complexity or semantic novelty of novel classes.

weaknesses.point: No analysis of confidence calibration, error modes, or bias introduced by synthetic data
weaknesses.evidence: The work lacks evaluation of calibration metrics on in-distribution data, qualitative error analysis, or discussion of potential biases and artifacts induced by LLM-generated OOD examples.

questions.question: How sensitive is CoNAL’s performance to the choice and quality of generated novel labels and examples?
questions.motivation: Understanding this is critical to assess robustness and guide practical generation strategies or alternative data sources.

questions.question: Can CoNAL generalize to truly novel classes outside LLM knowledge or to other NLP tasks beyond classification?
questions.motivation: Evaluating these would clarify the approach’s real-world applicability and limitations.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of comparisons to recent NLP-specific OOD detection methods. The paper omits comparisons to energy-based, Mahalanobis distance, spectral normalization, and other contemporary NLP OOD detection approaches, weakening claims of state-of-the-art performance
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient ablation studies on key components and hyperparameters. No ablation or quantitative analysis is provided on the impact of synonym filtering during novel label generation, contrastive loss hyperparameters, or the size and quality of generated novel examples
critique_points.point_003: Dependence on GPT-3 API raises reproducibility and stability concerns. Reliance on GPT-3 for label generation involves opaque, evolving model behavior and training data, complicating exact replication and wider adoption
critique_points.point_004: Limited dataset diversity and artificial open-set splits constrain generalizability. Experiments use only four datasets with artificial splits that may not reflect real-world open-set complexity or semantic novelty of novel classes
critique_points.point_005: No analysis of confidence calibration, error modes, or bias introduced by synthetic data. The work lacks evaluation of calibration metrics on in-distribution data, qualitative error analysis, or discussion of potential biases and artifacts induced by LLM-generated OOD examples
